topic: Stop building AI tools. Start building AI processes.
hook_summary: You're not an AI company. You're a business that needs leverage.

scenes.audio_text: Everyone's rushing to build AI tools. I think that's a mistake.
scenes.visual_prompt: @daniel, medium close-up, sitting in a modern coffee shop with warm natural light from a window. He's leaning forward, hands resting on a small table. Slight frown, serious eye contact. Locked-off camera, shallow depth of field, candid documentary style.

scenes.audio_text: I spent six months building a custom AI scheduling bot. It failed. Clients didn't care about the tech — they cared about getting booked faster.
scenes.visual_prompt: @daniel, walking on a city sidewalk, over-shoulder shot from behind. He's gesturing with one hand, wearing the same dark polo. Late afternoon golden hour light, slight motion blur on background. Real street environment with people passing.

scenes.audio_text: What actually worked? I took an off-the-shelf Zapier workflow, connected it to Calendly and Stripe, and reduced my admin time from 12 hours to 2 hours a week.
scenes.visual_prompt: @daniel, sitting at a desk in a home office, medium shot. He's looking at a laptop screen, but turns to speak to camera. Desk has a coffee mug and notebook. Natural light from a window, warm tones. Slow push-in.

scenes.audio_text: Stop building AI. Start automating your business. That's where the leverage is.
scenes.visual_prompt: @daniel, wide shot leaning against a brick wall outside, arms crossed. Direct eye contact, slight smile. Overcast soft lighting, no harsh shadows. Clean background, candid street photography style.
